steps.title: Write the savings equation for each person
steps.content:
The two lines on the graph correspond to Barbara's and Maria's savings. Barbara starts with 70 dollars and saves 10 dollars each week, so after $x$ weeks:
$$y = 70 + 10x$$
Maria starts with 120 dollars and saves 5 dollars each week, so after $x$ weeks:
$$y = 120 + 5x$$

steps.title: Set equations equal and solve for x
steps.content:
Their savings are equal at the intersection point of the two lines on the graph. Algebraically, set the expressions equal:
$$70 + 10x = 120 + 5x$$
Subtract $5x$ from both sides:
$$70 + 5x = 120$$
Subtract $70$ from both sides:
$$5x = 50$$
Divide by $5$:
$$x = 10$$
The graph confirms the lines cross at $x = 10$ weeks. That matches choice C — C is correct.
